channel.title: Quality of Life on VitaminDWiki
channel.description: Recent content in Quality of Life on VitaminDWiki

item.title: All 8 of the factors that increase health span are associated with Vitamin D
item.description:
• Life’s Essential 8: Higher scores extend health span
      • 8 related items in VitaminDWiki

Life’s Essential 8: Higher scores extend health span

MDEdge

based on Association of Cardiovascular Health With Life Expectancy Free of Cardiovascular Disease, Diabetes, Cancer, and Dementia in UK Adults - JAMA Intern Med. 2023;183(4):340-349. doi:10.1001/jamainternmed.2023.0015 Can be read at DeepDyve

Not smoking

    Stopping smoking increases Vitamin D

item.title: Extending Health Span is a better goal than extending Lifespan
item.description:
The economic value of targeting aging

Nature Aging volume 1, pages 616–623 (2021)

Andrew J. Scott, Martin Ellison & David A. Sinclair

Table of Contents

Developments in life expectancy and the growing emphasis on biological and ‘healthy’ aging raise a number of important questions for health scientists and economists alike. Is it preferable to make lives healthier by compressing morbidity, or longer by extending life? What are the gains from targeting aging itself compared to efforts to eradicate specific diseases? Here we analyze existing data to evaluate the economic value of increases in life expectancy, improvements in health and treatments that target aging.

item.title: SciAm - No benefit from more than 20 ng of Vitamin D (nope)
item.description:
Download the Scientific American PDF from VitaminDWiki

“National population sampling showed that most people were already getting enough of the vitamin”

“…most people probably don’t need supplements”

“…noting that the majority of the population is just fine at 16 ng/ml.”

“…Levels have actually risen since [2010]” Actually, levels have fallen

Describes the failure of 4 trials that used too little vitamin D

Does not mention the >700 successful Vitamin D Randomized Controled Trials (see below)

item.title: Happiest country on earth has a high Vitamin D level (Finland, with little sun)
item.description:
• Is Finland really the happiest country in the world? Finns weigh in.

Is Finland really the happiest country in the world? Finns weigh in.

Washington Post March 31. 2022

  • “For the fifth year in a row, Finland has been named the happiest country in the world by the United Nations-sponsored World Happiness Report.”

Note: Most of the top 10 happiest countries are in Europe and supplement or fortify with vitamin D

item.title: Asian men often smoke then die of lung cancer (if you must smoke, take Vitamin D) – meta-analysis
item.description:
Tobacco Smoking and Mortality in Asia. A Pooled Meta-analysis

JAMA Netw Open. 2019;2(3):e191474. doi:10.1001/jamanetworkopen.2019.1474

1,000,000 participants in 20 cohort studies in this Asian meta-analysis

  • Low Vitamin D is worse for your health than smoking

  • Each ng extra vitamin D associated with better breathing (and 2X better for smokers) – March 2018

item.title: Following hip fracture 2,000 IU of vitamin D daily improved quality of life
item.description:
Effects of a simple home exercise program and vitamin D supplementation on health-related quality of life after a hip fracture: a randomized controlled trial

Qual Life Res (2019). https://doi.org/10.1007/s11136-019-02100-4

K. Renerts K. Fischer B. Dawson-Hughes E. J. Orav G. FreystaetterH.-P. Simmen H.-C. Pape A. Egli R. TheilerH. A. Bischoff-Ferrari

Note: Daily vitamin D takes many months to achieve a level which provides a benefit

Note: This study did not notice a benefit until 6 months

item.title: Healthspan is more important than Lifespan – JAMA
item.description:
• From Lifespan to Healthspan - 2018
      • Might be able to “square the curve” with Vitamin D
      • 193 studies cited this study as of Dec 2023

From Lifespan to Healthspan - 2018

JAMA. 2018;320(13):1323-1324. doi:10.1001/jama.2018.12621

S. Jay Olshansky, PhD1

#### Chart from PDF

1900

  • Many deaths before age 3

  • Adult death rates inacreas at age 20, short lifetimes, peak at age 71

2016

  • Adult death rates increase at age 50, long lifetimes, peak at age 90
